states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Session started, waiting for user credentials

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User name provided, waiting for password

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Successfully logged in, ready for commands

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data channel parameters negotiated, waiting for transfer command

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: A data transfer operation (upload, download, or listing) is in progress

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source file identified, waiting for destination name

invariants_list.rule: LIST, RETR, STOR require prior successful authentication
QUIT terminates the session from any state
DATA commands typically require PASV or PORT first

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, CAPI, CDUP, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD, MODE
Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: RESETTING

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

fuzzing_observation.explanation: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, CAPI, CDUP, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD, MODE

invariants_created: LIST, RETR, STOR require prior successful authentication
QUIT terminates the session from any state
DATA commands typically require PASV or PORT first

status_changes.rationale: Response code 331 directly indicates user name is okay and needs a password.
Response code 230 directly indicates successful login and state transition to authenticated.
Response code 229 indicates entering extended passive mode.
Response code 200 confirms the active data port selection.
Response code 200 confirms the extended active data port selection.
Response code 221 terminates the session.
Response code 221 terminates the session from the pre-login state.
Heuristic trace suggests metadata retrieval maintains auth state.
Heuristic trace suggests metadata retrieval maintains auth state.
Heuristic trace suggests metadata retrieval maintains auth state.
Heuristic trace suggests MLSD can initiate data transfer directly.
Heuristic trace suggests STOR can initiate data transfer directly.
Heuristic trace suggests APPE can initiate data transfer directly.
Heuristic trace suggests transition through ready state.
Heuristic trace suggests transition through ready state.
Heuristic trace suggests return to auth state after MLSD.
Heuristic trace suggests return to auth state after RETR.
Heuristic trace suggests return to auth state after STOR.

status_changes.description: LIST, RETR, STOR require prior successful authentication
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP access control; data transfer commands are not permitted before the 230 login response.

status_changes.description: DATA commands typically require PASV or PORT first
status_changes.rationale: While some clients may attempt to reuse existing channels, standard operation requires defining a data port/mode before a transfer command like LIST or RETR.
